content.problemStatement: You are given integers NNN and KKK. Construct a connected undirected unweighted graph with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) such that there are exactly KKK unordered pairs of nodes which have distance equal to the diameter of the graph (the largest distance between two nodes), or determine that no such graph exists.
constraints: `Explanation**Example case 1:** The diameter of the graph is 333. There are exactly 444 unordered pairs of nodes at distance 333: (1,5)(1, 5)(1,5), (1,6)(1, 6)(1,6), (2,5)(2, 5)(2,5) and (2,6)(2, 6)(2,6).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If no valid graph exists, print a single line containing the integer âˆ’1-1âˆ’1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM â€” the number of edges in your graph.
Then, print MMM lines. Each of these lines must contain two space-separated integers uuu and vvv (1â‰¤u,vâ‰¤N1 \le u, v \le N1â‰¤u,vâ‰¤N; uâ‰ vu \neq vuî€ =v) denoting the endpoints of one edge. All edges must be different, i.e. parallel edges are not allowed.
